request.question: Does adding 250mM trehalose to freezing medium improve HeLa post-thaw viability vs DMSO?

What is the specific success threshold (e.g. >=15% improvement)?

hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Does adding 250mM trehalose to freezing medium improve HeLa post-thaw viability vs DMSO?

literature_qc.explanation: No retrieved papers cover the same intervention (250mM trehalose in freezing medium) applied specifically to HeLa cells, which is essential for an exact match. The papers primarily focus on trehalose in alternate biological systems (e.g., bull sperm, hematopoietic cells). Thus, the biological system and the specific context of the experiment are not aligned with the proposed hypothesis.
literature_qc.recommended_action: No directly matching publication found. This combination appears novel. Proceed with experiment planning.

plan.objective: To compare the post-thaw viability of HeLa cells cryopreserved in 250mM trehalose versus DMSO.
plan.experimental_design: HeLa cells will be cryopreserved using two different protocols: one with a trehalose-based freezing medium and one with DMSO. Each condition will be tested with three biological replicates to ensure accuracy and reproducibility.
controls: HeLa cells cryopreserved with DMSO in 10% v/v.
HeLa cells not subjected to cryopreservation (fresh control).

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 5 min | Cryopreservation Setup
Thaw 2 vials of HeLa cell stock (1 mL each) in a 37°C water bath for 2 min until just thawed.
Expected outcome: Complete thawing of cells with no ice crystals visible.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 10 min | Cryopreservation Setup
Transfer thawed HeLa cells to a 50 mL falcon tube and add 10 mL of complete DMEM medium containing 10% FBS dropwise over 5 min.
Expected outcome: Cells are in DMEM medium without clumping.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 10 min | Cryopreservation Setup
Centrifuge the cell suspension at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature to pellet the cells.
Expected outcome: Well-defined pellet of cells at the bottom of the tube.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 5 min | Cryopreservation Setup
Resuspend the cell pellet in 5 mL of trehalose freezing medium (250mM trehalose with 10% DMSO) for the trehalose group.
Expected outcome: Uniform suspension of cells without visible aggregates.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 5 min | Cryopreservation Setup
Prepare DMSO freezing medium by resuspending the pellet from the previous centrifugation in another 5 mL of 10% DMSO medium.
Expected outcome: Uniform suspension of cells without visible aggregates.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 5 min | Cryopreservation Setup
Aliquot 1 mL of the trehalose-containing cell suspension into one freezing vial and 1 mL of the DMSO-containing suspension into another freezing vial.
Expected outcome: Each vial contains separate treatments clearly labeled.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 5 min | Cryopreservation Setup
Place vials in Mr. Frosty freezing container and store at -80°C for 24 hours to allow gradual freezing.
Expected outcome: Vials are securely placed and gradually cooled.

DAY 1 | 5 min | Thawing Cells
After 24 hours, transfer vials from Mr. Frosty to a 37°C water bath and thaw for 2 min until just thawed.
Expected outcome: Cells thaw rapidly with no ice crystals seen.

DAY 1 | 10 min | Thawing Cells
Transfer the thawed cells to a 50 mL falcon tube and add 10 mL of complete DMEM medium dropwise over 5 min.
Expected outcome: Cells are diluted into growth medium without clumping.

DAY 1 | 10 min | Thawing Cells
Centrifuge the cells at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature to pellet cells.
Expected outcome: Well-defined pellet of cells at the bottom of the tube.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Thawing Cells
Resuspend the cell pellet in 10 mL of complete DMEM medium and incubate at 37°C for 30 min to recover before analysis.
Expected outcome: Cells are suspended in medium and ready for viability assessment.

DAY 1 | 15 min | Viability Assessment
Perform Trypan Blue exclusion assay by mixing 10 µL of cell suspension with 10 µL Trypan Blue solution and counting live/dead cells using a hemocytometer.
Expected outcome: Accurate count of live and dead cells, with viable cells >80%.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Data Analysis
Compare the percentages of viable cells between the trehalose and DMSO treated groups using ANOVA followed by post-hoc tests for significance.
Expected outcome: Statistical significance of viability comparisons with p-value < 0.05.

assumptions: Trehalose provides superior cryoprotection compared to DMSO for HeLa cells.
HeLa cells will survive the freezing and thawing process relatively intact and retain viability post-thaw.
Differences in viability will be detectable using Trypan Blue exclusion assay.
decision_criteria: Proceed if post-thaw viability in trehalose group is higher than 80%.
Proceed if viability in DMSO group is higher than 80%.
Consider results valid if mean difference in viability between groups is statistically significant (p<0.05).
reproducibility_notes: Use the same passage number (3-10) of HeLa cells for all experiments.
Ensure freezing and thawing protocols are consistently timed and executed across replicates.
Calibrate the hemocytometer and check the viability of Trypan Blue before use.
feedback_incorporated:
PRIOR SCIENTIST CORRECTIONS FOR SIMILAR EXPERIMENTS:
A scientist has previously flagged these specific issues on similar comparative_cryopreservation experiments. You MUST implement each correction below in your output unless it directly contradicts the current hypothesis:
1. replicate gate in plan text
2. Add Mr. Frosty step
3. add 30-minute post-thaw recovery at 37°C before trypan blue counting

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: higher than control improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To compare the post-thaw viability of HeLa cells cryopreserved in 250mM trehalose versus DMSO.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
